GitSetup.keywords: git, vcs, gitignore, branches, commits, hooks, history, tags, secrets, repo-bootstrap
GitSetup.Mission:
Foundational git policy for every project: gitignore baseline, branch strategy, commit convention, history hygiene, tag/release semantics, hooks integration, secrets discipline, repo bootstrap.
Goal: a repo that is reproducible, auditable, safe вЂ” no accidental secret leaks, no platform-specific binaries committed, no fabricated В«greenВ» status hiding broken history. The repo is the project's canonical record; every commit is part of the audit trail.
Scope is git itself. Tool choice for hooks runner (lefthook / husky / simple-git-hooks) is the `git-hooks` category in the infrastructure scope discovery session. CI provider is the `ci` category. Both distinct from this rule.

Belief_State.Axiom:
`.gitignore` covers three layers.
**Mandatory** (every project): - `.claude/settings.local.json` (personal Claude Code overrides; team-shared `.claude/settings.json` IS tracked). - `.env`, `.env.local`, `.env.*.local` (secrets; only `.env.example` with placeholders is tracked). - OS artifacts: `.DS_Store`, `Thumbs.db`, `desktop.ini`. - Editor caches: `.idea/`, `*.swp`, `.vscode/` (or selectively). - Coverage: `coverage/`, `*.lcov`, `.nyc_output/`. - Logs: `*.log`, `npm-debug.log*`, `yarn-error.log`, etc.
**Tool-derived** (per chosen stack from infrastructure scope discovery): - Node: `node_modules/`, `dist/`, `.next/`, `.nuxt/`, `.cache/`. - Python: `__pycache__/`, `*.pyc`, `.venv/`, `.pytest_cache/`, `.ruff_cache/`. - Go: `vendor/` (if not committed by policy), build artifacts. - Rust: `target/`.
**Environment-specific:** developer-team decision (e.g., `scripts/local/`).
Wrong here = secrets in repo, multi-GB bloat, platform-specific native binaries breaking CI.

Pick one strategy at project init, document in README, enforce via remote branch protection.
| Strategy | When | |---|---| | `trunk-based` (recommended default) | small teams + strong CI; short-lived branches off `main`, merged within a day | | `github-flow` | long-lived feature branches, PR-driven, no `develop` | | `git-flow` | planned release cadences; `main` + `develop` + `release/*` + `hotfix/*` |
Default branch name: `main` (not `master`) вЂ” both locally and on remote.
Branch naming: `/`, where `type в€€ {feature, fix, chore, docs, refactor, test, perf}`. Lowercase, kebab-case.
Mixing strategies within one team multiplies merge confusion.

Default: Conventional Commits format `(): `. Alternatives acceptable with documented rationale.
Conventional Commits: - Types: `feat`, `fix`, `docs`, `chore`, `refactor`, `test`, `perf`, `build`, `ci`, `style`, `revert`. - Subject: imperative mood, в‰¤72 chars, no trailing period. - Body (optional): wraps at 100 chars, blank line between subject and body. - Footer: `BREAKING CHANGE: вЂ¦`, issue refs, sign-off.
Example: `feat(auth): add OAuth2 PKCE flow for native clients`.
Acceptable alternatives (Decision Log entry required): - **plain** вЂ” no type prefix, just imperative subject. - **DCO sign-off** вЂ” `Signed-off-by: Name ` mandatory for licensing/legal; compatible with conventional commits.
Conventional Commits enables auto-changelog, semantic-release, machine-readable history.

Linear history via rebase or squash-merge for PRs. Force-push forbidden on shared branches.
- **Shared** (force-push FORBIDDEN): `main`, `develop`, `release/*`, any branch with active PRs from others. - **Personal** (force-push allowed): your own `feature/*`, `fix/*` until merged.
Merge strategies: - `squash-merge` вЂ” feature branch becomes single commit on main. Simple history; loses commit granularity. - `rebase-and-merge` вЂ” feature commits rebased onto main, no merge commit. Preserves granularity; requires clean local history. - `merge commit` вЂ” discouraged unless branch shape must be preserved (e.g., release branches).
Hooks bypass: `--no-verify` is FORBIDDEN unless operator explicitly authorizes; if used, the bypass MUST be documented in commit body.
Shared-branch force-push silently rewrites history collaborators have already pulled. Recovery is manual and error-prone.

Destructive git commands risk silent data loss. NOT blanket-forbidden вЂ” they're tools вЂ” but each invocation requires explicit operator authorization recorded in the Execution Log.
**Requires explicit auth** (status `[!] BLOCKED` until operator confirms): - `git reset --hard ` вЂ” discards working tree + index changes since ``. - `git restore .` (without specific path) вЂ” discards all uncommitted changes in working tree. - `git restore --staged --worktree ` with broad path вЂ” same risk. - `git clean -fd` / `-fdx` вЂ” deletes untracked files (and ignored-files with `-x`). - `git checkout -- ` (or `git checkout HEAD -- `) with broad path вЂ” discards uncommitted changes. - `git push --force` / `--force-with-lease` on shared branches. - `git branch -D ` (force-delete unmerged) вЂ” distinct from `-d` (safe delete). - `git rebase -i` вЂ” interactive history rewrite. - `git reflog expire` / `git gc --prune=now` вЂ” drops recoverable history. - `git filter-repo` / `git filter-branch` вЂ” bulk history rewrite.
**Protocol per invocation:** 1. State intent in Execution Log: В«Need to ; proposing .В» 2. Set status `[!] BLOCKED` per `AX_BLOCKER_ESCALATION` (in `task-execution`). 3. Wait for operator's explicit confirmation. 4. Only then run.
**Recovery first, destruction second.** Reflex В«let me reset --hard and start overВ» is the failure mode this axiom prevents. Recovery options: - `git reflog` вЂ” find lost commit/state. - `git stash` вЂ” park uncommitted changes safely. - `git revert` вЂ” inverse commit (preserves history). - `git checkout -- ` вЂ” restore one file from a known-good commit.
Forbidden as habit: В«let me just reset --hard and try againВ»; `git clean -fdx` to В«free diskВ» without listing what will be deleted; `--force-with-lease` on `main` because it В«sounds safer than --forceВ» (both destructive on shared branches).

Semver tags: `vMAJOR.MINOR.PATCH`. Annotated tags only.
Format: - Stable: `v1.2.3`. - Pre-release: `v1.2.3-alpha.1`, `v1.2.3-beta.2`, `v1.2.3-rc.1`. - Build metadata (optional, discouraged): `v1.2.3+20260507`.
Always annotated: ``` git tag -a v1.2.3 -m "Release 1.2.3 вЂ” " ``` Lightweight tags (`git tag v1.2.3` without `-a`) are FORBIDDEN вЂ” no metadata, look identical to branch refs.
Push explicitly: `git push origin v1.2.3` (or `--tags` after release).

Hooks fire on every commit/push. Required hooks: `pre-commit` (lint + typecheck on staged, <5s), `commit-msg` (conventional-commits validator), `pre-push` (full test suite). Optional: `prepare-commit-msg` (prepend issue ID), `post-merge` (reinstall if lock changed). Hook config committed to repo; one onboarding command registers them (e.g. `lefthook install`).

LFS for large binaries. Submodules discouraged.
**Git LFS** вЂ” required when tracking binary files >100MB (GitHub hard limit; other hosts vary). Configure `.gitattributes`: `*.psd filter=lfs diff=lfs merge=lfs -text`. LFS adds infrastructure cost (storage, bandwidth quota) вЂ” use only when binaries are necessary.
**Submodules** вЂ” discouraged in favor of monorepo or package dependencies. If unavoidable: pin to specific commit (not branch); document in README why; expect fragile workflow (`git submodule update --init --recursive` after every checkout).

Secrets never committed. On leak: rotate first, rewrite history second.
**Prevention:** - `.env`, `.env.local`, `.env.*.local` always gitignored (`AX_GITIGNORE_BASELINE`). - `.env.example` tracked with placeholder values to document required keys. - Pre-commit hook scans staged files for secret patterns (recommended: `gitleaks`, `detect-secrets`, `trufflehog`). - Code review checks for hardcoded credentials in any file extension.
**On leak detection:** 1. **Rotate the leaked credential immediately** (revoke + reissue). The credential is compromised the moment it hits a remote, regardless of subsequent rewrite. 2. Document incident: what leaked, when, who rotated, what notifications went out. 3. Optional: `git filter-repo` or BFG to scrub history, then force-push (coordinate with team).
Git history is forever. Once a secret reaches the remote, assume it's harvested by scanners within minutes. Rotation is mandatory; history rewrite is hygiene, not security.

Setup_Steps.Step: Run `git init -b main` (or `git init` + `git branch -m main`). Default branch is `main`, never `master`.

Author `.gitignore` **before staging any other file** вЂ” first push is the riskiest moment for leaks. Mandatory entries: `.claude/settings.local.json`, `.env`/`.env.local`/`.env.*.local`, `.DS_Store`, `Thumbs.db`, `.idea/`, `*.swp`, `coverage/`, `*.log`. Add tool-derived entries per chosen stack (see AX_GITIGNORE_BASELINE for full reference).

Choose one strategy вЂ” trunk-based (default for small teams with strong CI), github-flow (PR-driven), or git-flow (planned release cadences). Document in README. Immutable after first collaborator push.

Stage and commit foundational files only: `LICENSE`, `README.md`, `.gitignore`. Message: `chore: initial commit`. Never include `.env`, generated artifacts, `node_modules/`, or credentials in this or any commit.

Add remote and push only after `.gitignore` is committed and verified. Before first feature work, configure branch protection on `main`: require PR before merge, require CI status checks, disallow force-push.

Install hooks runner per infrastructure scope decision (lefthook / husky / simple-git-hooks). Commit config file (e.g. `lefthook.yml`). Register with one onboarding command. Configure: `pre-commit` (lint + typecheck on staged, target <5s), `commit-msg` (conventional commits), `pre-push` (full test suite).
Confirm: `git status` clean; `.gitignore` covers all tool-generated paths; hooks fire on test commit; remote branch protection active.

Anti_Pattern.Why_Bad: 10вЂ“500 MB bloat; macOS-compiled native binaries fail on Linux CI; PR diffs become unreadable. Lock file already encodes the dep tree deterministically.
Anti_Pattern.Good: `node_modules/` in `.gitignore`. `npm ci` / `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile` restores from lock file.

Anti_Pattern.Why_Bad: Silently rewrites history collaborators already pulled. Recovery requires every team member to reset their local branch вЂ” error-prone, sometimes impossible if uncommitted work is on top.
Anti_Pattern.Good: Force-push only on personal branches. Shared-branch issues fixed via revert commits.

Anti_Pattern.Bad: Committed `.env` with real API key, then `git rm` in next commit.
Anti_Pattern.Why_Bad: The secret is in history. Anyone can `git log -p` and recover it. Public repos expose to scanners within seconds.
Anti_Pattern.Good: Rotate the credential immediately. Then rewrite history with `git filter-repo` and force-push (coordinate with team). Add `.env` to `.gitignore` BEFORE first push.

Anti_Pattern.Bad: `git commit --no-verify` because pre-commit hook is В«too slowВ».
Anti_Pattern.Why_Bad: Hooks exist to catch mistakes locally. Routinely bypassing shifts mistakes into CI (slower, more expensive) or main (worse). Slow hook = fix the hook, don't skip it.
Anti_Pattern.Good: Diagnose hook slowness (lint scope too wide? full test suite in pre-commit instead of pre-push?). Optimize. `--no-verify` only with explicit operator authorization, documented in commit body.

Anti_Pattern.Why_Bad: No tagger / date / message metadata. Looks identical to a branch ref in some tools. Release lineage unrecoverable.
Anti_Pattern.Good: `git tag -a v1.2.3 -m "Release 1.2.3 вЂ” "`. Always annotated for release tags.

Hook.Command: git ls-files | grep -E '^\.env($|\.)' | grep -v '^\.env\.example$' | head -1 | grep -q . && echo "FAIL: .env tracked" || echo "OK"
git ls-files node_modules 2>/dev/null | head -1 | grep -q . && echo "FAIL: node_modules tracked" || echo "OK"
git symbolic-ref refs/remotes/origin/HEAD 2>/dev/null | grep -q 'main$' && echo "OK" || echo "FAIL: default branch not main"

Hook.Purpose: Verify all release tags are annotated (object type = tag, not commit).
Hook.Command: for t in $(git tag); do test "$(git cat-file -t $t)" = "tag" || echo "FAIL: $t is lightweight"; done; echo "OK"

GitSetup.Reward_Criteria:
вњ… `.gitignore` covers mandatory + tool-derived layers; first commit only after `.gitignore` in place. вњ… Default branch is `main`; remote branch protection prevents direct push, requires PR + green checks. вњ… Commits follow chosen convention; subjects в‰¤72 chars, imperative mood. вњ… Shared-branch history is linear; release tags annotated, semver format. вњ… Hooks runner installed via single onboarding command; `--no-verify` only with operator auth. вњ… No `.env*` (except `.env.example`) tracked; pre-commit secret scanner in place. вњ… Destructive commands preceded by explicit operator authorization in Execution Log; recovery preferred over destruction.
вќЊ Secrets in history; `node_modules/` (or equivalent) committed. вќЊ Force-push to shared branches; lightweight release tags. вќЊ `--no-verify` as habit; default branch named `master` in new project. вќЊ Destructive command run as В«let me just reset --hardВ» reflex without operator authorization. вќЊ `.claude/settings.local.json` tracked.
